# Schedule Auto-Generator UX Improvements — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Fix the confusion in `AutoGenerateDialog` (duplicate position names, opaque employee list, 120 single-line warnings without diagnosis, missing scope header) so a manager can understand at a glance what will be generated, whether the plan is feasible, and what action to take when something is missing.

**Architecture:** Single-dialog refactor touching three files — `auto-generate-dialog.tsx` (UI), `schedule-generator.ts` (emit structured warnings instead of raw strings), `types.ts` (new `AutoGenWarning` discriminated union). Data cleanup runs first as a SQL fix to remove the duplicate "Aux. Administrativo" ambiguity. No schema migrations.

**Tech Stack:** Next.js 14 App Router · React 18 · shadcn/ui + Tailwind v3 · `lucide-react` icons · Sonner toasts · Supabase client (browser).

---

## Verification strategy

This codebase has **no test runner** (per `CLAUDE.md`). Each task ends with manual browser verification against `npm run dev` (or `docker compose up` on port 3100). When a task's change is pure logic (generator warnings), a temporary `tsx` scratch file under `/tmp` exercises the function — documented in the step itself.

## File Structure

Files that will be modified:

| File | Responsibility after changes |
|------|------------------------------|
| `src/lib/types.ts` | Add `AutoGenWarning` discriminated union + group helpers. `AutoGenResult.warnings` becomes `AutoGenWarning[]` instead of `string[]`. |
| `src/lib/schedule-generator.ts` | Emit structured warnings (with `kind`, `positionId`, `date`, etc.) instead of pre-formatted strings. No algorithmic change. |
| `src/components/schedule/auto-generate-dialog.tsx` | Receive structured warnings, group them, show position-with-department labels, show employee context, add scope header, pre-flight capacity check, grouped warning cards with action links. |

No new files. No new migrations. All existing callers of `generateSchedule` only read `warnings.length` or render strings — touching both callers in lockstep with the type change.

---

## Task 0: Data cleanup — disambiguate duplicate "Aux. Administrativo"

**Why first:** as long as two rows in `positions` share the exact string "Aux. Administrativo", no UI label can distinguish them without repeating the department. Renaming is a one-off migration done via the Supabase MCP. After this, the UI changes are simpler because the names are already unambiguous.

**Files:**
- No code changes. Data-only migration via Supabase MCP (`apply_migration`) against project `ugkvuinkynvtuiutwlkd`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Confirm the two duplicate rows and which one has employees**

Run this SQL via Supabase MCP `execute_sql` on project `ugkvuinkynvtuiutwlkd`:

```sql
SELECT p.id, p.name, d.name AS departamento,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM profiles WHERE position_id = p.id) AS empleados
FROM positions p
LEFT JOIN departments d ON d.id = p.department_id
WHERE p.name = 'Aux. Administrativo'
ORDER BY p.id;
```

Expected: two rows, one with `departamento='Farmacia'` (has 3 empleados), one with `departamento='Recepción'` (has 0 empleados).

- [ ] **Step 2: Rename both to include department in the name**

Run via `apply_migration` with name `rename_duplicate_aux_administrativo`:

```sql
UPDATE positions
SET name = 'Aux. Administrativo (Farmacia)'
WHERE id = 'c0000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004';

UPDATE positions
SET name = 'Aux. Administrativo (Recepción)'
WHERE id = 'c0000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003';
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify the rename**

Re-run the Step 1 query. Expected: names now include department suffix.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit (no code changes — record the data migration in the repo)**

Create `supabase/migrations/012_rename_duplicate_aux_administrativo.sql` with the same UPDATE statements from Step 2, then:

```bash
git add supabase/migrations/012_rename_duplicate_aux_administrativo.sql
git commit -m "chore(db): rename duplicate 'Aux. Administrativo' positions to include department"
```

## Task 1: Introduce structured `AutoGenWarning` type

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/lib/types.ts` — add discriminated union, update `AutoGenResult`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the warning union and grouped view type**

Open `src/lib/types.ts`. At the bottom of the file (after `LaborConstraints`, before `EntryMap`), add:

```ts
// Auto-gen warnings are structured so the UI can group them by cause.
export type AutoGenWarning =
  | { kind: "no_employees_in_position"; positionId: string; date: string; shiftTemplateId: string }
  | { kind: "no_available_employee"; positionId: string; date: string; shiftTemplateId: string }
  | { kind: "no_templates_selected" }
  | { kind: "no_employees_selected" };

export interface AutoGenWarningGroup {
  kind: AutoGenWarning["kind"];
  count: number;
  affectedPositionIds: string[];
  affectedDates: string[];
  samples: AutoGenWarning[]; // first 5 for drill-down
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update `AutoGenResult` to use structured warnings**

In the same file, locate `AutoGenResult` and replace the `warnings` field:

```ts
export interface AutoGenResult {
  entries: Omit<ScheduleEntry, "id" | "created_at" | "updated_at" | "employee" | "position" | "shift_template">[];
  warnings: AutoGenWarning[];
  stats: Record<string, { shifts: number; hours: number }>;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Type-check the project**

Run: `npx tsc --noEmit`

Expected: errors in `schedule-generator.ts` (pushes strings into `warnings`) and `auto-generate-dialog.tsx` (renders `warnings` as strings). These errors confirm the type migration is wired correctly; we fix them in Tasks 2 and 3.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add src/lib/types.ts
git commit -m "feat(types): add structured AutoGenWarning union for schedule generator"
```

```ts
import type {
  AutoGenConfig,
  AutoGenResult,
  AutoGenWarning,
  ProfileWithPositions,
  ShiftTemplate,
  LaborConstraints,
  ScheduleEntry,
  StaffingRequirement,
} from "./types";
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Replace the early-return warning strings**

Find the block starting at line 215 with `const warnings: string[] = [];` and replace through line 230:

```ts
  const warnings: AutoGenWarning[] = [];
  const entries: AutoGenResult["entries"] = [];
  const stats: Record<string, { shifts: number; hours: number }> = {};

  // Selected employees & templates
  const selectedEmployees = employees.filter((e) => config.employeeIds.includes(e.id));
  const selectedTemplates = templates.filter((t) => config.shiftTemplateIds.includes(t.id));

  if (selectedTemplates.length === 0) {
    warnings.push({ kind: "no_templates_selected" });
    return { entries, warnings, stats };
  }
  if (selectedEmployees.length === 0) {
    warnings.push({ kind: "no_employees_selected" });
    return { entries, warnings, stats };
  }
```

At line 14, replace:

```tsx

```

- [ ] **Step 2: Extend type import**

At lines 21-30, replace the type import block with:

```tsx
import type {
  Profile,
  ProfileWithPositions,
  ShiftTemplate,
  Position,
  Schedule,
  ScheduleEntry,
  StaffingRequirement,
  AutoGenResult,
  AutoGenWarning,
} from "@/lib/types";
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Import `Link` and `MONTHS`-like helpers are already imported — nothing to add**

Confirm line 19 already imports `MONTHS`. No change needed.

- [ ] **Step 4: Add a warning-grouping helper above the component**

Right after the existing `import ... from "@/lib/types";` block (after line 30, before `interface AutoGenerateDialogProps`), add:

```tsx
type WarningGroup = {
  kind: AutoGenWarning["kind"];
  count: number;
  affectedPositions: Set<string>;
  sampleDates: string[];
};

function groupWarnings(warnings: AutoGenWarning[]): WarningGroup[] {
  const map = new Map<AutoGenWarning["kind"], WarningGroup>();
  for (const w of warnings) {
    let g = map.get(w.kind);
    if (!g) {
      g = { kind: w.kind, count: 0, affectedPositions: new Set(), sampleDates: [] };
      map.set(w.kind, g);
    }
    g.count++;
    if ("positionId" in w) g.affectedPositions.add(w.positionId);
    if ("date" in w && g.sampleDates.length < 3 && !g.sampleDates.includes(w.date)) {
      g.sampleDates.push(w.date);
    }
  }
  return Array.from(map.values()).sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count);
}
```

              </div>
            )}

## Task 7: Pre-flight capacity check

**Why:** Warn the user *before* generating when the demand exceeds the selected employees' weekly capacity, so they don't sit through a generation only to see 120 warnings.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/schedule/auto-generate-dialog.tsx` — add a capacity-estimate component between Employees and Constraints

- [ ] **Step 1: Add capacity calculation helper**

Just above the `return (` in the component (around line 208, after `computeEquityStdDev`), add:

```tsx
  function estimateCapacity() {
    const selectedEmps = employees.filter((e) => selEmployees.has(e.id));
    const selectedTpls = shiftTemplates.filter((t) => selTemplates.has(t.id));
    if (selectedEmps.length === 0 || selectedTpls.length === 0) return null;

    const daysInMonth = new Date(year, month + 1, 0).getDate();
    const weeksInMonth = daysInMonth / 7;

    // Supply: sum of min(constraint, emp cap) weekly hours × weeks
    const weeklyCap = selectedEmps.reduce(
      (sum, e) => sum + Math.min(constraints.maxHoursPerWeek, e.max_hours_per_week),
      0
    );
    const supplyHours = weeklyCap * weeksInMonth;

    // Demand: N positions × N templates × daysInMonth × avg template hours (fallback mode)
    const avgTplHours =
      selectedTpls.reduce((s, t) => {
        const [sh, sm] = t.start_time.split(":").map(Number);
        const [eh, em] = t.end_time.split(":").map(Number);
        let min = eh * 60 + em - (sh * 60 + sm);
        if (min < 0) min += 24 * 60;
        return s + (min - t.break_minutes) / 60;
      }, 0) / selectedTpls.length;

    const slots = useDemand
      ? 0 // cannot cheaply estimate demand-mode; skip in that case
      : selPositions.size * selectedTpls.length * daysInMonth;
    const demandHours = slots * avgTplHours;

    if (slots === 0) return null;
    const ratio = supplyHours / demandHours;
    return { supplyHours, demandHours, slots, ratio };
  }
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Render the capacity banner**

Locate the `{/* Constraints info */}` block around line 320. Insert this *above* it (so capacity appears right after the Employees section and before the constraints line):

```tsx
            {/* Pre-flight capacity estimate */}
            {(() => {
              const cap = estimateCapacity();
              if (!cap) return null;
              const pct = Math.round(cap.ratio * 100);
              const tone =
                cap.ratio >= 1
                  ? "border-emerald-200 bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-800"
                  : cap.ratio >= 0.6
                  ? "border-amber-200 bg-amber-50 text-amber-800"
                  : "border-red-200 bg-red-50 text-red-800";
              return (
                <div className={`rounded border p-2 text-xs ${tone}`}>
                  <div className="font-medium">
                    {cap.ratio >= 1
                      ? `Capacidad suficiente (${pct}%)`
                      : `Cobertura estimada: ${pct}%`}
                  </div>
                  <div className="opacity-80">
                    {cap.slots} ranuras (~{Math.round(cap.demandHours)}h) vs {Math.round(cap.supplyHours)}h disponibles.
                    {cap.ratio < 1 && " Sumar personal o reducir plantillas."}
                  </div>
                </div>
              );
            })()}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Type-check + lint**

Run: `npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint`

Expected: no errors.

- [ ] **Step 4: Manual verify**

With 3 empleados (40h cada uno) × 3 plantillas × 2 posiciones × 30 días → demand ≈ 1440h, supply ≈ 480h, pct ≈ 33%. Expected: red banner saying "Cobertura estimada: 33%".

Deselect one posición (back to 1) — demand halves to ~720h, pct ≈ 66%. Expected: amber banner.

                const filled = result.entries.length;

                  (w) => w.kind === "no_employees_in_position" || w.kind === "no_available_employee"

## Task 9: Disable position pills with zero eligible employees

**Why:** Prevents the user from selecting a position that is guaranteed to produce "no_employees_in_position" warnings.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/schedule/auto-generate-dialog.tsx` — the position pills render in Task 4

- [ ] **Step 1: Build an eligibility map**

Below the `groupWarnings` helper (added in Task 3, Step 4), add:

```tsx
function eligibleEmployeeCount(
  positionId: string,
  employees: Profile[],
  secondaryMap: Map<string, Set<string>>
): number {
  let n = 0;
  for (const e of employees) {
    if (e.position_id === positionId) n++;
    else if (secondaryMap.get(e.id)?.has(positionId)) n++;
  }
  return n;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Fetch secondary positions on dialog open (same pattern as `handleGenerate`)**

Inside the component, add state + effect near line 78 (right after the existing `useEffect` that fetches staffing):

```tsx
  const [secondaryMap, setSecondaryMap] = useState<Map<string, Set<string>>>(new Map());

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!open) return;
    async function fetchSecondary() {
      const { data } = await supabase
        .from("employee_secondary_positions")
        .select("employee_id, position_id")
        .in("employee_id", employees.map((e) => e.id));
      const m = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
      for (const row of data ?? []) {
        if (!m.has(row.employee_id)) m.set(row.employee_id, new Set());
        m.get(row.employee_id)!.add(row.position_id);
      }
      setSecondaryMap(m);
    }
    fetchSecondary();
  }, [open, supabase, employees]);
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Extend the position pill render (from Task 4) to disable + de-select zero-eligible positions**

Replace the `.map((p) => { ... })` block from Task 4 with:

```tsx
                  {positions.map((p) => {
                    const dept = (p.department as { name?: string } | undefined)?.name;
                    const selected = selPositions.has(p.id);
                    const eligibles = eligibleEmployeeCount(p.id, employees, secondaryMap);
                    const disabled = eligibles === 0;
                    return (
                      <button
                        key={p.id}
                        type="button"
                        disabled={disabled}
                        title={disabled ? "Sin empleados asignados" : undefined}
                        onClick={() => {
                          if (disabled) return;
                          setSelPositions(toggleSet(selPositions, p.id));
                        }}
                        className={`rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-xs font-medium border transition-colors text-left ${
                          disabled
                            ? "bg-muted text-muted-foreground/50 border-dashed border-border cursor-not-allowed"
                            : selected
                            ? "border-transparent text-white"
                            : "bg-background text-muted-foreground border-border"
                        }`}
                        style={!disabled && selected ? { backgroundColor: p.color } : undefined}
                      >
                        <div>{p.name}</div>
                        <div className={`text-[10px] ${selected ? "opacity-80" : "text-muted-foreground/70"}`}>
                          {dept ? `${dept} · ` : ""}
                          {disabled ? "sin empleados" : `${eligibles} elegibles`}
                        </div>
                      </button>
                    );
                  })}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: De-select disabled positions when the map loads**

In the same `useEffect` added in Step 2, after setting the secondary map, filter `selPositions`:

```tsx
      setSecondaryMap(m);
      setSelPositions((prev) => {
        const next = new Set<string>();
        for (const id of prev) {
          const eligibles = positions.reduce((acc, p) => {
            if (p.id !== id) return acc;
            return acc + employees.filter(
              (e) => e.position_id === id || m.get(e.id)?.has(id)
            ).length;
          }, 0);
          if (eligibles > 0) next.add(id);
        }
        return next;
      });
```

Expected: no errors.

Generate the horario. Expected: now **zero** "no_employees_in_position" warnings — only the capacity-bound "no_available_employee" group (if any).

## Self-review checklist (done before handoff)

- **Spec coverage:** all 10 recommendations covered — #1 deduplicación visual (Task 0 + 4) · #2 contexto empleado (Task 5) · #3 header scope (Task 6) · #4 pre-flight (Task 7) · #5 posiciones sin empleados deshabilitadas (Task 9) · #6 agrupar advertencias (Task 3) · #7 headline orientado a decisión (Task 8) · #8 contextualizar equidad (Task 8) · #9 botones de acción (Task 3) · #10 renombrar duplicados (Task 0).
- **Placeholders:** none — every code step has final code ready to paste.
- **Type consistency:** `AutoGenWarning` union defined in Task 1, consumed consistently in Tasks 2 and 3. `groupWarnings` helper signature matches. `eligibleEmployeeCount` signature stable from Task 9 onward.
- **No test runner caveat:** every task has a manual-verification step with expected observable outcome in the browser.
